treatments.name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib (BRAF/MEK Inhibition)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 ROAR basket study (n=36) with 4-year follow-up. FDA-approved for BRAF V600E-mutant ATC in 2018. Not a Phase 3 RCT but sufficient evidence for tissue-agnostic/rare cancer approval

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 14.5 months vs historical 5 months. 12-month OS rate 51.7%. ORR 56% with 8% CR. Median DOR 14.4 months. Transforms a uniformly fatal disease into one with meaningful survival

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since May 2018 for BRAF V600E-mutant ATC. Widely available at all oncology centers. Standard of care per NCCN guidelines

safety_profile.rationale: 75% treatment-related AEs. Pyrexia 47%, anemia 36%, fatigue/nausea 33%. 17% permanent discontinuation, 47% dose reduction. Manageable but significant toxicity burden, concerning for ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient is BRAF V600E+ — this is the required biomarker. Perfect match for the mechanism of action

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dabrafenib selectively inhibits BRAF V600E kinase, blocking constitutive activation of the MAPK/ERK signaling pathway. Trametinib inhibits MEK1/2 downstream, providing dual pathway blockade that prevents resistance through pathway reactivation.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (May 2018) — standard of care for BRAF V600E+ ATC

treatments.name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib + Pembrolizumab (Triple Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 multicenter trial data published 2025. Retrospective comparisons show superiority over doublet. Not yet Phase 3 confirmed but rapidly becoming standard approach

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 20 months (95% CI: 12.6-NR) vs 9.0 months for D+T alone. 1-year OS 71%, 2-year OS 48%. Enables surgical resection in previously unresectable disease. Dramatically exceeds historical 5-month median

accessibility.rationale: Individual components are FDA-approved. Combination used off-label at major cancer centers. Phase 2 trials demonstrate feasibility. Increasingly considered standard approach for BRAF+ ATC

safety_profile.rationale: Triple therapy adds immune-related AEs to BRAF/MEK toxicity. Higher rate of grade 3+ events. Requires close monitoring. May be challenging for ECOG 2 patient though benefits likely outweigh risks

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has both BRAF V600E+ (for D+T) and PD-L1 high (for pembrolizumab). Optimal biomarker profile for triple combination

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines BRAF/MEK pathway inhibition with PD-1 checkpoint blockade. BRAF inhibition increases tumor antigen presentation, T-cell infiltration, and PD-L1 expression, creating synergy with anti-PD-1 therapy. The immunogenic tumor microenvironment change amplifies checkpoint inhibitor efficacy.

key_evidence.study_name: Neoadjuvant DTP for BRAF-mutated ATC Phase 2

biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 expression (benefit enhanced with high PD-L1)
treatments.availability: Phase 2 completed — off-label combination at major centers; increasingly adopted as standard

treatments.name: Vemurafenib + Cobimetinib + Atezolizumab (VCA Triple Therapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 single-center trial at MD Anderson (NCT03181100). BRAF-mutated cohort n=18. Longest OS reported for systematic therapy in ATC. Used as standard at MD Anderson

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 43.2 months in BRAF V600E cohort — unprecedented in ATC. Median PFS 13.93 months. ORR 50%. Represents the longest median OS ever reported for systematic therapy in ATC

accessibility.rationale: Available primarily at MD Anderson and select centers. Components are individually FDA-approved but combination is off-label for ATC. Requires specialized oncology center

safety_profile.rationale: Triple combination with known toxicity profiles. Vemurafenib dose reduction needed after atezolizumab addition. Immune-related plus targeted therapy AEs. Challenging for ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires BRAF V600E mutation — patient is positive. PD-L1 high status adds rationale for anti-PD-L1 component. Ideal biomarker profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vemurafenib inhibits BRAF V600E, cobimetinib inhibits MEK downstream, and atezolizumab blocks PD-L1. The BRAF/MEK inhibitor run-in period creates a favorable immune microenvironment before adding checkpoint blockade, maximizing synergistic anti-tumor immune activation.
treatments.availability: Phase 2 — available at MD Anderson; off-label at select centers

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy (Anti-PD-1)

evidence_level.rationale: Systematic review and meta-analysis (2025) pooling multiple studies. JCO publication on PD-1 blockade in ATC. FDA-approved for TMB-high or MSI-high tumors (tissue-agnostic)

survival_benefit.rationale: Pooled ORR 46%, DCR 74%. Some patients achieve durable CR lasting >2 years. All long-term responders had PD-L1 TPS >50% or high TMB — matching this patient's PD-L1 high profile

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved (tissue-agnostic for TMB-high). Widely available globally. Can be prescribed off-label for PD-L1 high ATC based on clinical evidence

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune checkpoint inhibitor profile. Manageable immune-related AEs. Better tolerated than combination regimens. Suitable for ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has PD-L1 high expression. All durable responders in ATC had PD-L1 TPS >50%. Excellent biomarker match for this patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody blocking PD-1 on T cells, preventing PD-L1/PD-L2-mediated immune evasion. In ATC, high PD-L1 expression and tumor mutational burden create a favorable immunogenic environment for checkpoint blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: Pembrolizumab in ATC Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

treatments.availability: FDA Approved (tissue-agnostic for TMB-high; off-label for PD-L1 high ATC)

treatments.name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data and retrospective cohort studies. ORR 51.9% in ATC. First-line effectiveness data published. Not Phase 3 but growing evidence base

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 11-18.5 months. Median PFS 10 months. 12-month PFS rate 41.2%, 12-month OS rate 44%. In PD-L1+ patients, response rate reaches 75%. Meaningful improvement over historical ATC outcomes

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs are FDA-approved individually. Combination used off-label in ATC. Lenvatinib FDA-approved for differentiated thyroid cancer. Widely available

safety_profile.rationale: Lenvatinib has significant toxicity: hypertension, proteinuria, diarrhea, fatigue. Combined with pembrolizumab immune AEs. Dose modifications frequently needed. ECOG 2 status is a concern

biomarker_match.rationale: Does not require BRAF mutation. PD-L1 high status predicts enhanced pembrolizumab benefit. Applicable as alternative or second-line if BRAF-targeted therapy fails

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib is a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT, inhibiting angiogenesis and tumor growth. Combined with pembrolizumab PD-1 blockade, the anti-angiogenic effect modulates the tumor microenvironment to enhance immunotherapy efficacy.

key_evidence.study_name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in ATC retrospective cohort

biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 expression (enhanced benefit with PD-L1 high)
treatments.availability: Off-label combination — both components FDA-approved individually

treatments.name: Multimodal Therapy: Surgical Debulking + Chemoradiation

evidence_level.rationale: Established multimodal approach supported by NCCN guidelines, retrospective series, and decades of clinical experience. No Phase 3 RCTs in ATC due to rarity

survival_benefit.rationale: Trimodality (surgery + chemoradiation) achieves median OS approximately 10 months, significantly better than single modality. Best outcomes in patients without distant metastases receiving >=50 Gy. However, limited benefit without targeted therapy

accessibility.rationale: Available at all cancer centers. Surgery, radiation, and chemotherapy are standard oncologic modalities. NCCN-recommended approach

safety_profile.rationale: Major surgery with risk of recurrent laryngeal nerve injury, airway complications. Chemoradiation adds mucositis, esophagitis, myelosuppression. Significant morbidity especially in ECOG 2 patient with airway compression

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement — universally applicable. However, BRAF V600E status now enables neoadjuvant targeted therapy to convert unresectable to resectable disease

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Surgical debulking removes bulk tumor to relieve airway/esophageal compression and reduce tumor burden. Hyperfractionated accelerated EBRT (typically 55-60 Gy) provides locoregional control. Concurrent doxorubicin or cisplatin radiosensitizes tumor cells.
treatments.availability: Standard of Care — available at all cancer centers per NCCN guidelines

treatments.name: Weekly Paclitaxel Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trials and nationwide prospective study. Established role as radiosensitizer and standalone chemotherapy in ATC. NCCN-listed option

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 21-53% depending on study and infusion schedule. Median OS approximately 6 months. 96-hour infusion showed 53% response rate but weekly schedule more practical. Moderate benefit as monotherapy

accessibility.rationale: Widely available, inexpensive generic chemotherapy. Can be administered at any oncology center. Standard part of ATC treatment protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Myelosuppression, peripheral neuropathy, alopecia, nausea. Weekly schedule better tolerated than 96-hour infusion. Manageable but adds toxicity burden to ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement — universally applicable cytotoxic chemotherapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Paclitaxel stabilizes microtubules, preventing depolymerization during cell division, leading to mitotic arrest and apoptosis. Weekly dosing maintains therapeutic drug levels while reducing peak toxicity compared to every-3-week dosing.

key_evidence.study_name: CATCHIT Group Phase 2 / Nationwide Prospective Study

treatments.availability: Widely available — NCCN-listed option for ATC

treatments.name: External Beam Radiation Therapy (IMRT/Hyperfractionated)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN-recommended component of multimodal therapy. Multiple retrospective series. Hyperfractionated accelerated regimens established for locoregional control

survival_benefit.rationale: Patients receiving >=50 Gy had superior survival, especially those without distant metastases. IMRT median dose 60 Gy shows better locoregional control. Important for palliation of airway compression. Rarely curative alone

accessibility.rationale: IMRT widely available at radiation oncology centers. Hyperfractionated schedules may require specialized planning but feasible at most academic centers

safety_profile.rationale: Radiation esophagitis, mucositis, skin toxicity, airway edema (dangerous in patient with existing compression). Requires careful planning to protect airway and spinal cord

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement — applicable to all ATC patients regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: IMRT delivers conformal radiation doses to the thyroid tumor bed and involved lymph nodes while sparing surrounding structures. Hyperfractionated accelerated schedules (twice daily) exploit the rapid proliferation rate of ATC cells to maximize tumor cell kill.

key_evidence.journal: Thyroid / Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism

treatments.availability: Standard of Care — widely available, NCCN recommended

treatments.name: Larotrectinib / Entrectinib (NTRK Inhibitors — if NTRK Fusion Present)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA-approved tissue-agnostic for NTRK fusion+ cancers. Pooled thyroid cancer data: ORR 71% overall, 29% in ATC specifically. Long-term follow-up with 48-month OS rate 72%

survival_benefit.rationale: In NTRK+ thyroid cancer overall: 24-month OS rate 76%, median OS not reached at 58 months. However, ATC-specific ORR only 14-29%. Dramatic benefit if NTRK fusion present but not tested in this patient

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved tissue-agnostic. Both larotrectinib and entrectinib widely available. Requires NTRK fusion testing to confirm eligibility

safety_profile.rationale: Excellent safety: most AEs grade 1-2. Only 7% grade 3+ related AEs. Very well tolerated — suitable for ECOG 2 patient if eligible

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires NTRK fusion — not identified in this patient's molecular profile (BRAF V600E, TP53, TERT). NTRK fusions and BRAF mutations are typically mutually exclusive. Very unlikely to be applicable

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Larotrectinib and entrectinib selectively inhibit tropomyosin receptor kinases (TRK) A, B, and C encoded by NTRK1/2/3 genes. In NTRK fusion-positive cancers, these fusions drive constitutive TRK activation. Inhibition blocks downstream RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT signaling.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of the Endocrine Society / JCO

biomarker_requirements: NTRK gene fusion (NTRK1, NTRK2, or NTRK3)

treatments.name: Lenvatinib Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 HOPE trial and meta-analysis of 10 studies. FDA-approved for differentiated thyroid cancer. Used off-label in ATC with documented activity

survival_benefit.rationale: Limited efficacy as monotherapy in ATC: pooled median OS 3.16 months, median PFS 3.16 months. ORR 15%, DCR 63%. 1-year OS only 11.9-22.2%. Better than BSC but inferior to BRAF-targeted or combination approaches

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for thyroid cancer. Widely available. Oral administration. Can be initiated quickly without requiring specialized center

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: hypertension (68%), diarrhea, decreased appetite, proteinuria, fatigue. Dose reductions frequently needed. Challenging in ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required — multi-kinase inhibitor. Broadly applicable. However, inferior to BRAF-targeted therapy for this BRAF V600E+ patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib is a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis, disrupts tumor vasculature, and directly suppresses tumor cell proliferation through multiple kinase pathways.

key_evidence.journal: European Journal of Cancer / Frontiers in Endocrinology

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for thyroid cancer — off-label for ATC

treatments.name: Sorafenib (Multi-Kinase Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial in ATC (n=20). FDA-approved for differentiated thyroid cancer. Documented activity in ATC subset, particularly those with papillary dedifferentiation history

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 3.9 months. PR rate only 10%, SD 25%. Two prolonged PR cases associated with papillary transformation history — relevant to this patient's PTC dedifferentiation. Limited overall benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for thyroid cancer. Oral administration. Widely available globally. Can be started quickly

safety_profile.rationale: Predictable and manageable toxicity. Hand-foot syndrome, diarrhea, fatigue. Generally better tolerated than cytotoxic chemotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor without specific biomarker requirement. Subgroup with papillary dedifferentiation (matching this patient's history) showed better responses. Inferior to selective BRAF inhibition for V600E+ patients

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sorafenib inhibits multiple kinases including RAF (including BRAF), VEGFR2/3, PDGFR-beta, c-KIT, FLT-3, and RET. Dual anti-proliferative and anti-angiogenic activity. Less selective than dabrafenib for BRAF V600E but broader kinase coverage.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Trial of Sorafenib in Advanced ATC

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for differentiated thyroid cancer — off-label for ATC

treatments.name: Doxorubicin + Cisplatin Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Established since 1985 for ATC. Multiple retrospective studies. Historically the backbone of ATC chemotherapy. NCCN-listed option

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS approximately 6 months. ORR 18-26% for combination (vs 5% doxorubicin alone). Modest benefit but provides radiosensitization. Largely superseded by targeted therapies for BRAF+ patients

accessibility.rationale: Universally available, inexpensive generic chemotherapy. Can be administered at any oncology center globally

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: cardiotoxicity (cumulative doxorubicin), nephrotoxicity (cisplatin), severe myelosuppression, nausea/vomiting. Poor tolerability in ECOG 2 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement — applicable regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II, causing double-strand breaks. Cisplatin forms DNA crosslinks preventing replication. Combined cytotoxic effect targets rapidly dividing ATC cells and serves as radiosensitizer in chemoradiation protocols.
treatments.availability: Widely available — NCCN-listed standard chemotherapy option

treatments.name: Chemo-Immunotherapy: Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab

evidence_level.rationale: Case series and emerging reports in 2025. Remarkable responses documented. Rationale supported by immunogenic cell death induced by chemotherapy enhancing checkpoint inhibitor efficacy

survival_benefit.rationale: Remarkable responses reported in case series. Combines cytotoxic activity of paclitaxel with immune activation of pembrolizumab. Potential option for patients progressing on BRAF-targeted therapy

accessibility.rationale: Both components individually FDA-approved and widely available. Combination can be used off-label. No specialized center required

safety_profile.rationale: Combined chemotherapy and immunotherapy toxicity. Myelosuppression from paclitaxel plus immune AEs from pembrolizumab. Monitoring required

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 high expression in this patient supports pembrolizumab benefit. Paclitaxel does not require biomarker selection. Good option for non-BRAF-directed combination

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Paclitaxel induces immunogenic cell death releasing tumor antigens and damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs), while pembrolizumab removes PD-1-mediated immune suppression. The combination converts immunologically cold tumors to hot by creating a pro-inflammatory microenvironment.
treatments.availability: Off-label combination — both drugs widely available

treatments.name: Sapanisertib (Dual TORC1/2 Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 clinical trial data. In early development for ATC. Promising preclinical rationale targeting mTOR pathway frequently activated in ATC

survival_benefit.rationale: Early phase data — no mature survival results specific to ATC. Activity demonstrated in NRF2-mutated tumors. Potential benefit in TP53-mutated ATC through alternative pathway targeting

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trials. Investigational compound. Limited sites

safety_profile.rationale: Oral targeted therapy with manageable side effect profile. Metabolic effects (hyperglycemia) require monitoring. Better tolerated than cytotoxic chemotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets mTOR pathway — relevant in ATC. May address resistance to BRAF/MEK inhibition through PI3K/AKT/mTOR crosstalk. TP53 mutation in this patient may enhance mTOR pathway dependence

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sapanisertib is a dual TORC1/TORC2 inhibitor that blocks mTOR signaling, a key survival pathway in aggressive thyroid cancers. Unlike rapamycin analogs that only inhibit TORC1, dual inhibition prevents compensatory TORC2 activation, more completely suppressing tumor proliferation.

key_evidence.study_name: Sapanisertib Phase 1/2 in Advanced Solid Tumors

treatments.name: AIC100 (CAR-T Cell Therapy Targeting ICAM-1)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 clinical trial by AffyImmune Therapeutics. Preclinical and early clinical data for ATC. Novel approach for solid tumors

survival_benefit.rationale: Very early phase — no mature survival data. Preclinical models show anti-tumor activity. Represents a potentially transformative approach if effective in solid tumors like ATC

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1 trial only. CAR-T manufacturing is complex, expensive, and available at very few specialized centers. Significant logistic barriers

safety_profile.rationale: CAR-T therapy carries risks of cytokine release syndrome and neurotoxicity. ICAM-1 expression on normal tissues raises on-target/off-tumor concerns. Early safety data needed

biomarker_match.rationale: ICAM-1 is highly expressed on ATC cells. Universal target for ATC regardless of driver mutation. Applicable to this patient's tumor

treatments.mechanism_of_action: AIC100 is a CAR-T cell therapy engineered to target ICAM-1 (CD54), a cell surface protein highly expressed on anaplastic thyroid carcinoma cells. Autologous T cells are modified to express chimeric antigen receptors recognizing ICAM-1, enabling direct tumor cell killing.

clinical_trials.title: Vemurafenib + Cobimetinib + Atezolizumab in BRAF V600E-Mutated ATC
clinical_trials.early_results: Median OS 43.2 months in BRAF-mutated cohort, ORR 50%. Longest OS reported for systematic ATC therapy

clinical_trials.title: Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab + Dabrafenib + Trametinib for BRAF V600E-Mutated ATC
clinical_trials.early_results: Median OS 20 months (95% CI: 12.6-NR); 1-year OS 71%, 2-year OS 48%. Enables surgical resection in previously unresectable disease

clinical_trials.title: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in Anaplastic and Poorly Differentiated Thyroid Carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 51.9%, median OS 18.5 months. PD-L1+ patients: ORR 75%

clinical_trials.title: Cemiplimab + Dabrafenib + Trametinib for BRAF-Mutated ATC
clinical_trials.early_results: Testing whether adding cemiplimab anti-PD-1 to standard BRAF/MEK improves outcomes over dabrafenib+trametinib alone

clinical_trials.title: AIC100 CAR-T Therapy Targeting ICAM-1 in ATC
clinical_trials.early_results: First-in-human CAR-T trial for ATC. Preclinical activity against ICAM-1+ ATC cells demonstrated

clinical_trials.title: Sapanisertib (Dual TORC1/2 Inhibitor) in Advanced Thyroid Cancers
biomarker_requirements: mTOR pathway activation or KEAP1/NRF2 mutation
clinical_trials.early_results: Preclinical rationale for ATC targeting through mTOR pathway; early clinical data pending

clinical_trials.title: Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocyte (TIL) Therapy in Solid Tumors Including ATC
clinical_trials.early_results: TIL therapy exploring efficacy in aggressive solid tumors including ATC with high TMB/PD-L1

combination_strategies.rationale: BRAF/MEK inhibition increases tumor antigen presentation and T-cell infiltration, creating synergy with anti-PD-1 therapy. Median OS 20 months (triple) vs 9 months (doublet). Patient's BRAF V600E+ and PD-L1 high status make this the ideal combination. Now considered standard of care for BRAF+ ATC at major centers

Alternative BRAF/MEK backbone with anti-PD-L1 checkpoint blockade. Achieved unprecedented median OS 43.2 months in BRAF-mutated ATC. Run-in period with targeted therapy before adding immunotherapy optimizes immune microenvironment conditioning

Lenvatinib anti-angiogenic effects remodel tumor vasculature to enhance immune cell infiltration. ORR 52%, median OS 18.5 months. In PD-L1+ patients, response rate reaches 75%. Alternative for patients who cannot tolerate BRAF/MEK inhibitors or as second-line therapy

Neoadjuvant BRAF/MEK inhibition converts unresectable ATC to resectable disease. Surgical resection after neoadjuvant therapy decreases locoregional complications, improves QoL, and prolongs survival. Critical for this patient with T4b disease and airway compression

Paclitaxel induces immunogenic cell death releasing tumor antigens, enhancing pembrolizumab efficacy. Remarkable responses reported in ATC. Option for patients progressing on BRAF-targeted therapy or non-BRAF-mutated tumors

supportive_care.evidence: PMC review of airway management in ATC (2020). NCCN guidelines recommend immediate airway evaluation for all ATC patients with compression symptoms
supportive_care.benefit: Prevention of acute airway obstruction, which is the primary cause of death in ATC. Humidity, short-term corticosteroids for acute management. Tracheal stenting preferred over tracheostomy when possible. Prophylactic stenting may prevent airway crisis. Critical for this patient with documented airway compression

supportive_care.evidence: Temel et al. (NEJM 2010) demonstrated OS benefit with early palliative care. ATC-specific guidelines emphasize early palliative discussions given aggressive disease course
supportive_care.benefit: Improved symptom management, quality of life, and advance care planning. Critical for establishing code status, nutritional preferences, airway management goals, and advance directives given ATC's aggressive course. Reduces ER visits and unplanned hospitalizations

supportive_care.approach: Nutritional Support and Gastrostomy Tube Consideration
supportive_care.evidence: ATC frequently causes esophageal compression and dysphagia. Gastrostomy tube placement recommended for nutritional support when esophageal involvement exists
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nutritional status during treatment, prevents cachexia, enables drug administration. Critical for patient tolerance of systemic therapy. Early placement before complete esophageal obstruction recommended

supportive_care.approach: Thyroid Hormone Replacement and TSH Suppression
supportive_care.evidence: Post-thyroidectomy or radiation-induced hypothyroidism requires replacement. TSH suppression may slow differentiated components. Added complexity with immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced thyroiditis
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents symptomatic hypothyroidism, maintains metabolic function during treatment. Monitor closely with immunotherapy due to high rate of immune-mediated thyroiditis. Levothyroxine dosing requires frequent adjustment

supportive_care.evidence: Short-term dexamethasone used for acute airway compression and radiation-induced edema. Standard supportive measure in ATC management guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Rapid reduction of peritumoral edema and airway inflammation. Temporizing measure while initiating definitive therapy. May reduce need for emergency tracheostomy. Important bridge to targeted therapy response

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines. ATC diagnosis carries extreme psychological burden due to poor prognosis and rapid symptom progression
supportive_care.benefit: Improved treatment adherence, reduced distress, better quality of life. Depression screening and management critical. Support for family and caregivers given aggressive disease trajectory

NCCN Guidelines Thyroid Carcinoma Version 1.2025

TERT Promoter Mutation Prognosis in ATC (Scientific Reports 2025)
ATC Contemporary Review of Challenges and Advances (Endocrine Practice 2025)
